message:
fix(scheduler/three_lane): gate drain certificate observations on actual drain phases

The drain progress certificate was being fed Lyapunov potential observations
on every governor tick, including during normal steady-state operation. This
caused false stall detection: routine potential fluctuations during non-drain
phases would trigger the stall_threshold and incorrectly escalate to
DrainObligations mode.

Fix: only feed the certificate when the Lyapunov governor's suggestion
indicates an active drain phase (DrainObligations or DrainRegions). When
the governor returns to NoPreference, reset the certificate so stale
observations from a prior drain cycle don't carry over.

This also simplifies the drain-certificate override logic: since the
certificate only produces verdicts during drain phases, the Quiescent
verdict can unconditionally relax back to NoPreference (the certificate
will be reset on the next non-drain tick). The old code had redundant
suggestion-matching guards that are no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
